assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command with Saturday maintenance window using --maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA" for weekend-only auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: Shows complete gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-end, and --maintenance-window-recurrence flags for weekly Saturday schedule

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides specific quarterly code freeze exclusions with example dates covering approximately 21-day periods (March 15 - April 5, etc.)

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command for November audit exclusion covering the entire month (2024-11-01 to 2024-11-30)

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Does not mention the 30-day maximum limitation for maintenance exclusions or explain how to handle longer freeze periods

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: While it shows manual upgrade procedures, it doesn't explicitly explain that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows and only auto-upgrades respect them

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends using the Stable channel and explains its benefits for compliance environments requiring stability

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Shows complete gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time flags for each exclusion type

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly warn about the risks of falling behind on versions during extended exclusion periods, though it mentions version End of Support tracking
